entry.title: Manual migrate XSLT Mapping from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In this blog, we check out how we can manually migrate XSLT mapping from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite.

The intention is to give an overview and entry point if you require, besides the well-known tools, manual intervention for a mapping.

Background: 

  • We have an original XSLT mapping on PO containing a lot of includes as well as java coding. Taking the original XSLT and ZIP from ESR won’t potentially work out of the box.

Approach

  • Bring the includes and path to the same level as the main XSLT mapping itself.
BeforeAfter

 

 

  • Check XSLT mapping and includes for Java functions and adjust the namespace as in the example below:
  • Change: new namespace based on the Java class name, exclude namespace from result, adjust function call with the namespace.
  • Important: simple Java functions should work. If you have complex Java functions like LookUp, please consider a redesign with Groovy, etc.
BeforeAfter
  • Create a new JAR file containing the Java class files according to the namespace path in the XSLT mapping.
  • Recommendation: Use NWDS/Eclipse for the setup of the JAR file
BeforeAfter

 

 

  • Upload the XSLT mapping and includes to "References" in your Integration flow (as a ZIP or via multi-selection in the upload dialog).

  • Upload the "JAR" File also to "References" in your Integration flow

 

Conclusion

  • As long as it is possible, consider using the migration tool for an automatic migration from PI/PO.
  • If a manual adjustment is required, this may give you an entry point for your investigation on XSLT mapping.
  • Keep in consideration: Migration is okay; however, modernization of your integration landscape and architecture also leads to better business outcomes

Further links:

Enable Migration Assessment Application 

Use the Migration Assessment Application 

Migration Guide for SAP Process Orchestration

entry.title: custom login page for identity authentication
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have created a full stack application in which i use SAP CAP with node.js and UI5 for ui and i also add XSUAA for authentication. 

I have also add SAP Cloud Identity service for multiple user login.
After the deployment of application i am able to access the application with "Default identity provider" an

d also with "Custom" (from Cloud identity services). 

after choosing "custom" i will redirect to CIS idp for login.

 

So i am able to customize this login page for only CSS and tenent text from the CIS account.

My query is that, is it possible that i can use my custom build login page instead of this login page ?

Any suggestion and recommendation would be helpful.

entry.title: Why was "external code" and "enabled for ESS" removed from the Available Time Type UI
entry.content:
Why was "external code" and "enabled for ESS" removed from the Available Time Type UI, when these fields are displayed immediately underneath in the Available Allowance Type UI?

It's inconsistent, and adds extra steps when configuring new time types in the time profiles.

It needs the basic configuration in the background for PLM process

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/6f020371ca9f4bbb8ec6e59e21da2e98/c8915c93b7634a399a17d95e1fd71b5b.html

In same Path define the below - Defining User Status Profiles

entry.title: Re: Enabling In-Order processing with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh Partitioned Queues
entry.content:
Hi @Hari-Rangarajan ,
While the blog focuses on partitioned queues, it seems to indicate that you can only achieve EOIO with PQs, which is not the complete picture.

Both PQs or Exclusive queues can be used for that purpose.
But I'm missing one other key aspect:
More important are other settings like the windowing, to only have one single message in-flight, to avoid the messages passing each other on the transport and handling retries correctly in the application layer (CPI in this case). We generally recommend that if one needs to maintain ordering across redeliveries, one should set the queue's max-delivered-unacked-msgs-per-flow to 1.

Why is this missing? Is this not necessary with PQs and CPI?

How can I do this?

entry.title: Dynamically Hiding Table Columns in Print Forms with Adobe LiveCycle Designer and JavaScript
entry.content:
Print Form Blogs

This blog is part of a series dedicated to print form adaptation with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. If you are interested in this topic, I recommend checking out my other blogs on the following list and my live session about print forms!

List of print form blogs:

  • How to get XML data for local preview of print forms without output history
  • Print Form Adaptation in Sales/Service 2: Get XML data for preview in Adobe LiveCycle Designer
  • Data Binding Basics in Print Forms
  • Data Binding with Repeating Items and Simple Tables in Print Forms
  • Data Binding with Repeating Items and Complex Tables in Print Forms
  • Building Tables with Scripts
  • Dynamically Hiding Table Columns in Print Forms with Adobe LiveCycle Designer and JavaScript (this blog)

 

Introduction

Most print forms that you work on with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer contain tables. While designing tables and binding data correctly can sometimes already be a challenge, I want to dedicate this blog to adapting tables during runtime, or to be more specific: How we can hide columns dynamically based on the data that they contain (or do not contain).

I wrote a whole blog post about that topic because it turned out that it's not as easy as one probably expects. The major part of this blog is the description of the journey, how I approached this task, what I discovered, and how I finally solved it. If you are only interested in the solution, you can directly jump to the respective section.

Before we deep dive into the topic, let's have a look at sample that I'll use to explain the backgrounds and demonstrate the solution.

 

The Sample

The sample consists of a very simple print form that has a table tblTable with a header row hdrHeader and a body row rowMain. The header row is enabled to be displayed on the initial page and on subsequent pages (Object palette -> Pagination). The body row is bound to a repeating data item "$.FormQuote.Item[*]" and is enabled to be repeated per data item (Object palette -> Binding; see the previous screenshot).

The data structure for this example has been kept as simple as possible. As we want to focus on items, it doesn't contain much more. The following screenshot shows the beginning of the XML sample file that I use. The actual number of items doesn't matter, as long as they are enough to achieve a page break, which also depends on the height of your body row. Preparing the sample data to force a page break is an important step in testing the print form, because tables can have a different "behavior" on subsequent pages as we will see in this blog.

 

The First Approach (Not Sufficient!)

During design time, the Designer allows you to insert and delete columns. But if you would like to hide a table column permanently (rather then removing it), you won't find an option. The first approach would probably be to select the column (which is possible by clicking slightly above the column while the cursor changes to a down pointing arrow) and set its presence to "Hidden (Exclude From Layout)". However, this is disabled for table controls as the following screenshot shows:

Interestingly, although the Designer doesn't allow you to do this during design time, you can still do it during runtime using a script - and that's actually what we want, isn't it? Before we look closer at this approach, let's discuss first, how tables are structured internally.

A table in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer's object model is a special aggregation of subforms and other controls such as text fields or draws ("labels") that form the rows and cells. There is no column object as such. However, we can access all the cells of a column, meaning the cell in the header row and all cells of the body rows (due to data binding, the rendered form has several body rows).

The following FormCalc script placed in the initialize or ready:form event of the tblTable object of the sample shown above, hides the header cell and all body cells of the Description column:

 

$.hdrHeader.hdrDescription.presence = "hidden"
$.rowMain[*].colDescription.presence = "hidden"

 

Keep in mind that rowMain is bound to a repeating data item and hence exists multiple times in the final form structure when it's rendered. Hence we need to address the cells of all these rows. FormCalc lets us easily address them by simply writing rowMain[*]. JavaScript doesn't allow that and we would need a for-loop instead.

I have seen this approach with hiding the column cells quite often. The reason for that might be the combination of the typical approach (setting something to "hidden") and the fact that it works... up to a certain extent!

This approach works only as long as the table doesn't cross a page break or at least doesn't repeat the header row in such a case. Another downside of this approach is: The width of the columns gets shifted. This might not be a problem when all columns have the same size. In the example, I intentionally used columns of different sizes to point out the problem. The following screenshot shows the result of this approach in the Preview. Compare the header of both pages with the previous screenshot in the Design view.

You can clearly see that the Description column has been "successfully" hidden, but the Quantity column now shows up with that size. Moreover, when looking at the second page in the Preview, we can see that the Description header cell is shown again, but has the Quantity body cells underneath it, and the Unit header cell stands out without body cells under it.

Now you could say: When the body rows get repeated and we must use [*] to access them, we may need to apply the same pattern to the header row as this gets repeated on subsequent pages as well. Resulting in something like this: $.hdrHeader[*].hdrDescription.presence = "hidden"

Unfortunately, this doesn't change anything and is maybe a reason, why the Designer doesn't allow you to set table cells to "Hidden". Hence, we need another solution.

Let's have a closer look at the issue with the changed column widths first and find a way how we can tackle that part of the issue. How can we influence the width of a column? Maybe this gives us further insights on how to work with columns? Spoiler alert: It does!

Changing a Column's Width

We'll first concentrate at the design-time and then at the scripting approach. When you want to change the width of a column in the Design view, you can select a single cell of the column and change the width from the Layout palette. This changes the width of the whole column.

Changing the column width via scripting, works differently and is the first time, we work column-oriented. This becomes more obvious when selecting the table object in the Hierarchy window and activating the XML Source view. (In case you don't see the XML Source tab yet, you can enable it from the application menu View -> XML Source. After enabling it from that menu, the tab will stay visible.)

In the XML Source view, you can see that tblTable is a special subform with the layout "table". The attribute columnWidths contains the width for each column as the previous screenshot shows. That means, in contrast to the design view, where you can influence the column width from a cell, the object model stores the width for each column "centrally" on the table level from where it can also be changed. Especially when working with complex tables, the Designer sometimes doesn't allow you to change the column width by dragging and dropping the column or even from the Layou palette. In such cases, you can usually change the column width from the XML Source view.

Caution: If you make a mistake while editing the XML and the Designer can no longer load it, the template is destroyed (at least as long as you can't fix it on your own). For that reason, I suggest to create a backup of the xdp-file before working in the XML Source.

Also, pay attention to the amount of values that the columnWidths attribute contains. In some print forms, I found more values than the table had columns, which can lead to the problem that the Designer doesn't allow you to edit the widths via the Layout palette or when it allows you to do so, some unexpected things may happen.

Coming back to the scriping approach: When you want to change the width of a column via scripting, you can access the same attribute via the object model. The following line of code changes the width of the second column from 40mm to 30mm:

tblTable.columnWidths = "15mm 30mm 100mm 18mm 30mm";

Hiding Columns

With the columnWidths attribute, we can not only change the width of columns, it is finally also the way to hide them! Have you tried to set the value for a single column to 0mm ? Sounds promising, but this only puts the column into an auto-size mode. However, the correct value is very close: 0.01mm 😁

With 0.01mm, the column becomes "hidden". You can also assign this value to a cell via the Layout palette and the column will disappear. So, this is exactly what we want and we can even apply this with a script! Is this the solution we were looking for? Not entirely yet... There is another problem with this approach: If the hidden column contains body cells that are allowed to automatically grow (e.g. because they also allow line breaks within their content), you will face the following result:

What happens here? The sample data intentionally includes a multi line Description and hence the sample form is designed to deal with that. The Description cell has the following two options enabled:

  • Object palette -> Cell -> ✔️Allow Multiple Lines
  • Layout palette -> Height -> ✔️Expand to fit

Setting the Description column's size to 0.01mm doesn't mean, it's completely excluded from the layout as the presence "Hidden" would do. Instead, the Designer tries to display the Description with approximately one letter per line, because there isn't enough space with a width of 0.01mm, although the column is not really visible. This leads to these grown row heights.

But of course, there is also a solution for this problem: We must remove the content from "hidden" cell's by setting their rawValue to "".

To summarize that: In order to dynamically hide a column, we must change the columnWidths attribute, which requires some string operations for splitting and replacing the measurement values and we need to remove the content of all affected cells. That sounds like a task where a script snippet would be very welcome...

 

The Solution - Script: HideTableColumn

The JavaScript snippet below can be used to hide table columns dynamically. Unfortunately, it looks a bit crowded in the source block below due to forced line breaks. The parameters of the function are explained as part of the script comments and I'll explain how to use the script based on the sample below. As I had an additional requirement to only hide a certain table column, if all body cells were empty, the script also covers this case.

 

//Hides columns of a table. Place it in the ready:form event of the table, e.g.: HideTableColumn(this, 1, "rowMain[*].colProduct");
// !! Make sure that the header cells of the columns that are to be hidden are not allowed to expand their height! To ensure that, go to XML view and ensure that the field has "h" instead of "minH" !!
// oTable: Table object where the column belongs to -> simply "this" when the script call is placed in the table object itself
// columnIndex: Zero based index of the table column that is to be hidden
// somBodyCells: String containing the SOM Expression to the body cells (containing [*]) of the column relative to the table object, e.g. "rowMain[*].colDescription"
// onlyHideIfAllEmpty: bool -> if true, column will only be hidden if all cells are empty, otherwise column will be hidden in all cases
// returns true if the column has been hidden
function HideTableColumn(oTable, columnIndex, somBodyCells, onlyHideIfAllEmpty)
{
	var oBodyNodes = oTable.resolveNodes(somBodyCells);
	
	if (onlyHideIfAllEmpty)
	{
		for(var i = 0; i < oBodyNodes.length; i++)
		{
			var oField = oBodyNodes.item(i);
		 	// on the first occurence of any content, leave the function
			if (oField.rawValue > "")
				return false;
		}
	}
	
	for(var i = 0; i < oBodyNodes.length; i++)
	{
		// set the value to ""; setting the presence to "hidden" would scramble columns; "invisible" would introduce line breaks and expand heights
		oBodyNodes.item(i).rawValue = "";
	}	

	// setting columnWidth is necessary to hide the column of all subsequent pages
	var aWidths = oTable.columnWidths.split(" ");
	var newWidths = "";
	
	for (var i = 0; i < aWidths.length; i++)
	{
		if (i == columnIndex)
			newWidths += "0.01mm "; // hide a column
		else
			newWidths += aWidths[i] + " ";
	}
	
	oTable.columnWidths = newWidths;
		
	return true;
}

 

The script should be added to a scripting object to make it possible to call it from different places across the print form. Even if you need it only in a single place and could put it into the event where you also call it, my suggestion is to use a script object for "reuse" functions.

To add a script object, right click on the root element in the Hierarchy window and select Insert Script Object as the following screenshot shows. You can give the script object a name, for instance "utils".

Once the script snippet has been pasted in the script object, you can take care of calling the script. My suggestion is to do this in the ready:form event of the table where you want to hide the columns. In the sample case, we want to hide the Description column of the table tblTable, hence the calling script would look as follows (Make sure to choose JavaScript as the scripting language!):

 

// FormQuoteNotification.bdyMain.frmTable.tblTable::ready:form - (JavaScript, server)

utils.HideTableColumn(this, 2, "rowMain[*].colDescription", false);

 

When calling scripts that are part of a script object, you must include the script object's name. In my case, I have named the script object "utils", hence I have to call the function with utils.HideTableColumn(...);.

The parameters explained:

  • The first parameter oTable requires the table object. When you place the script in an event of the table, you can simply write this.
  • The second parameter columnIndex requires the zero-based column index. This will be used to split the columnWidths string and replace the respective part with 0.01mm. Hiding the third column means index 2.
  • The third parameter somBodyCells requires a string containing a so called SOM expression, which is the path to the body cell in the object model relative to the table, including the [*] notation, because we will have several body rows after rendering.
  • The last parameter onlyHideIfAllEmpty requires a boolean value (true/false) and specifies if the column should only be hidden, if all body cells are empty. That means "false" hides the column in all cases. "true" makes the script check if any cell contains text and if so, the column will be left visible.

Remark on the used event: Calling the script during the ready:form event, was the best solution in my tests. It also works when you call it in the initialize event of the table. In this case, there's only one special behavior that you should keep in mind: The script sets the rawValue of the affected body cells to "". When you do this during the initialize event, this is reflected in the data source! That means, if you have another table where you want to display the values of this column, this won't show values either. This behavior does not happen when you place the script in the ready:form event.

 

Summary

Dynamically hiding a table column in a print form with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer isn't as straight forward as one may wish. When I was looking for ways to hide columns, I was hoping for something like table.column[x].hidden = true;, but that doesn't exist.

The script of this blog post is my way to accomplish this task and has worked so far with the requirements I had. But I wouldn't be surprised if there's a much easier and better solution and I just didn't find it. If you know a better alternative, kindly let me know! 😉For everyone else, feel free to use the script and drop me some feedback! I hope it helps! 🙂

 

Want To Learn More? -> Live Sessions

I deliver monthly live sessions with a changing agenda on the topic of print form adaptation in different SAP solutions (currently SAP Sales and Service Cloud (the former SAP Cloud for Customer), SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2, SAP Business ByDesign and SAP S/4HANA Cloud). The main part of the session is usually solution independent, as I work in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, but I also mention solution specifics in every session. If you are interested and would like to join, here are the details:

Starting 2024 the live sessions have moved to a new platform: You can find them via this new direct link on learning.sap.com. You still need a corresponding subscription in order to register for the live session.

If you want to get informed about the next blogs of this series or updates on the live session, please follow this blog post by clicking on the green “follow” button on the left. You can also follow me fwys for more blog posts about SAP Customer Experience Solutions – Integration and Extensibility topics.

entry.title: Extract TPART Business Partner Functions (Texts) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_TPART_CUSTOMER          

  •  Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_TPART_CUSTOMER   
  • Tcode - Z_TPART_CUST    
  • Entity Description - Business Partner Functions (Texts) Data 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- TPART.CSV  
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – TPART, KNVP, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • VTEXT 

------ABAP PROGRAM START FROM HERE------------

REPORT Z_TPART_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\TPART.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNVP-KUNNR,
VTEXT like TPART-VTEXT,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT DISTINCT KNVP~KUNNR
TPART~VTEXT
KNB1~BUKRS
FROM KNVP INNER JOIN KNB1
ON KNVP~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR INNER JOIN TPART ON TPART~PARVW = KNVP~PARVW
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Re: Chart data label auto change color disabled in design...
entry.content: Hi Stefan, could you try starting from scratch? Create a new story, then create a new theme, change the chart data label, and apply the changes. Then, check whether the reset button is available.

entry.title: Dynamically change the input type of a Parameters Variable.
entry.content:
Due to a particular requirement if a Basis layer decides to switch to a new table(Example: swu_wlscan2) instead of an old table(Example: swu_wlscan ) and thereby changing the value range of some of its Parameters(Example: Job Suffix), the application layer functionalities that use this table needs to adapt. The challenge here is that in application layer you are not sure that in a particular environment which basis release and support package is applicable and which of the 2 tables is active. In this case, if you have a program(Example/SAPSRM/OFFLINEAPPROVALSEND) in which there is Parameters declared for selection-screen and this Parameters type is that of the changed parameter in the Basis release, then there is problem statement of how to dynamically have this parameter type determined depending on the availability of the old or new table in Basis release. So that the application layer does not have to create a new program altogether to cater to the table changes.

You can solve the issue by

  1.     First replacing the static type declaration of Parameters to dynamic

Example:

     PARAMETERS: p_jobsuf LIKE SWU_WLSCAN-CURR_NUMB.

      Replace above with below statement

      PARAMETERS: p_jobsuf LIKE (lv_type). 

2.     Find the type of Dynamic type (Example:lv_type) in Initialization event block by checking the existence of the table.

INITIALIZATION.

CALL FUNCTION 'DD_EXIST_TABLE'
EXPORTING
  tabname      = 'SWU_WLSCAN2'
  status       = 'A'
IMPORTING
  subrc        = lv_subrc
EXCEPTIONS
  wrong_status = 1
  OTHERS       = 2.
IF lv_subrc <> 0.
  lv_type = 'SWU_WLSCAN-CURR_NUMB'.
ELSE.
  lv_type = 'SWU_WLSCAN2-CURR_NUMB'.
ENDIF.

I've two problem and I am unable to find blog or documentations on it.

Is there a way to assign the Dac object so the row level security to user groups ?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - Setting up Multitenancy & using Extensibili...
entry.content:
Which is the correct way to implement multitenancy?

We recommend to follow the documentation in Capire

The documentation describes using a separate sidecar for the deployment so that the main application is not affected by the load that additional subscriptions might create. As mentioned by @alperdedeoglu , you can skip it if you do not expect many subscriptions.

Is there a way to use Extensibility in API directory?

The SusaaS example implements a second microservice in the API directory that deploys its database tables to the same database container as the main application. This is possible if you can ensure that both deployments do not interfere with each other. If you can make sure that the model is consistent.

However, CAP MTXS currently has no support for "service dependent"  extensions, meaning different extensions for different applications deploying to the same database container.

In this case, this means that extensions created for the API service would be loaded for the main application as well. This could cause compile errors because the main application might not have all entities in its model that have been extended for the API service.

entry.title: Re: when should an income statement GL account NOT have a...
entry.content:
Hello @mwfrizzell in general all p/l GL's are created as cost element but expenses/revenue of non-operating (not related to core business of company) nature can be excluded as cost element like sale of asset, some interest on FD (In S4 while creating GL there is option of non -operating type as well if select this no need to create that GL as cost element).

Apart from that WIP P/L GL, COGS (in costing based COPA) are also not created as cost elements. Please refer below thread apart from WIP P/L GL others are optional can or cannot be created as cost element.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/when-not-to-create-cost-elements-for-p-l-accounts-implications/qaq-p/7190742

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Wrocław, PL)
entry.content:
The binding registration for this SAP CodeJam is at: https://career.leverx.com/external-events/sap-codejam 

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

You'll become acquainted with how embedded Machine Learning in SAP HANA can be used to build ML models. In working through this CodeJam you'll also become familiar with how the SAP Business Application Studio can be used to work with Jupyter notebooks.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Wednesday, October 30th in Wrocław, Poland 🇵🇱 from 10:00 to 16:00 CEST (UTC+2). The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is a Developer Advocate Vitaliy Rudnytskiy.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/hana-ml-py-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

The event is being hosted by LeverX at
LABO | Coworking
Grodzka 9, 50-137 Wrocław

Note that spaces are limited and you must register via https://career.leverx.com/external-events/sap-codejam. 

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Hello Stefan,

First of all, I would like to thank you for this very interesting and well written blog post.

We are currently facing some important performance issue for a customer in the utilities industry and would like to implement some summarization function to resolve this issue.

Here is the context:

  • The customer comes from a system conversion from SAP ECC
  • Due to the customer industry, we are using SAP IS-U with some FI-CA (contract accounting) and CO-PA (margin analysis)
  • We face some important data volumes when we transfer the FI-CA postings to GL
  • It came so far, that some standard GUI transactions can no longer be used due to time outs
  • Our goal is to reduce significantly the numbers of line items generated from FI-CA to GL for BSEG table and ACDOCA table
  • The data volume comes mostly from CO-PA characteristics

 

We have tried the following in Sandbox environment:

  • Configuration of “Independent Summarization of Entry View” – BSEG
  • Configuration of “Independent Summarization of General Ledger View and Profitablity Segments” COPA / ACDOCA
  • Configuration of “Dependent Summarization of General Ledger View”
  • All these configurations point are available under Financial Accounting --> Financial Accounting --> lobal Settings --> Document --> Summarization

 

Results in tables BSEG and ACDOCA:

  • PSG no longer available in entry view
  • In ACDOCA table the fields maintained in customizing were initialized as expected but after that, the line items where not summarized.

Are we missing out on something? Our goal is really to reduce the number of line items that are generated because of CO-PA characteristics. The system seems to initialize the fields correctly but does not perform any summarization after that. Any idea or clue could help us out.

Kind regards,

Samuel

entry.title: SAP Group Reporting Data Collection: Advanced Monitoring and Alerting of Your Imports
entry.content:
The Data Mapping apps in SAP Group Reporting Data Collection automates the data load process in group reporting. It connects to multiple external sources, such as ERP systems, performs data transformation, and loads the data into SAP S/4HANA for group reporting. Data mapping is key to acquiring data in a controlled, automated and scalable way. 

We’re excited to announce the release of advanced monitoring and alerting for your import process with SAP Group Reporting Data Collection. You can now monitor all job runs in a single view and benefit from new capabilities including run status, visual display of errors, warnings, information and successes in micro-charts, and direct access to log information. Within the same app, you can also notify users by email depending on the job run status.  

Key features include: 

  • Understand the status of your jobs at a glance 
  • Get detailed information on your job runs  
  • Improved UX – filter, sort, freeze
  • One-click access to logs and files 
  • Notification and alerting  

--
Understand the status of your jobs at a glance


SAP Group Reporting Data Collection - Run Data Mapping page

When you open the “Run Data Mapping” app in SAP Group Reporting Data Collection, you can see the status of your last job runs at once. Statuses can be red, orange, or green: error, warning, or success. This monitoring page gives you a consolidated view of the status of your import jobs. 

In addition to the statuses, you get qualitative information on the status of the source, mapped, and imported data. If your import job is in error, you can quickly see where it comes from: source data not mapped, issue during the transformation of the mapping process, issue during the data import into SAP S/4HANA for group reporting. 

For source data, mapped data, or imported data, you can directly see the proportion of errors, warnings or information compared to successes in the associated micro-chart. To get the exact numbers, for example, 1 error and 90 successes for a given job, just mouse over the micro-chart, as illustrated in the screenshot below.


--
Get detailed information on your job runs


In the same monitoring page, you get detailed information on the job runs, such as: 

  • Overall job run time – “Run Duration” 
  • Time to transform data between source and target – “Mapping Duration” 
  • Mapping Status and Import Status 
  • Test import (yes/no)  
  • Source and target 

 You also see precise information on the data sent to SAP S/4HANA for group reporting: 

  • Consolidation version (for example actuals, forecast, budget, or restatements) 
  • Consolidation units 
  • Fiscal year and period 
  • Chart of accounts 

You can tailor the page to your needs and choose from 47 different information columns to characterize your job runs. Save your personal settings as a view that you can re-apply later. 

--
Improved UX: Filter, Sort, Freeze

Filtering

(1) The filter bar helps you set criteria to limit the number of jobs displayed on the page. You can save your personal settings as a view that you can re-apply later. 

(2) You can filter by various criteria that are proposed by default: status, last run on, version, fiscal year and period, or consolidation unit. As per the last run on, you can select a specific date or time range. 

Imagine you want to quickly find all job runs which failed: just set the filter criteria to easily display only the job runs with the "Error" status. This allows you to identify and address any issues efficiently. Also, you can focus on jobs that generated data for one or multiple consolidation units. Just use the “Consolidation Units” criteria to narrow down the page. 

(3) You can adapt the filter bar according to your needs. In addition to the criteria that are proposed by default in the filter bar, use the “Adapt Filters” to choose from 28 filtering dimensions, control their visibility and order.

Sorting and freezing

By default, to make your user experience as practical as possible, you see the most recent job runs at the top of the page, and the default freeze shows the first four columns. You can modify these settings, and save them as a personal view. 

Exporting 

If needed, you can export the new monitoring page to a spreadsheet.  

--
One-click access to logs and files

We’ve improved SAP Group Reporting Data Collection’s user experience for monitoring your data load process. Now, you can access or download the logs and files with one click. This helps you quickly analyze job runs and ensure the integrity, quality, completeness, compliance, and reliability of financial data. 

() Click the date link in the “Last Run On” column to directly access details on: 

  • The transformation between source and target data – mapping log  
  • The import of mapped data into group reporting – import log 

(2) Click the task log link in the “Task Log” column to see all data collection records in group reporting. 

(3) Download the traceability log of the last job run from the “Traceability Log” column. This allows you to analyze mapping log and import log entries more deeply, trace generated amounts back to the source data and the mapping rules that generated them. 

(4) With the data mapping app, you can also generate a CSV file as a target. From the new monitoring page, you can download the CSV target file generated by your last job run with one click from the “CSV Target File” column. 

--
Notification and alerting

With the notification feature, users are notified when an import job finishes, depending on its status. For example: 

  • An admin user can be alerted when a job run fail in error 
  • A finance business user can be alerted when a job successfully imports data 

From the jobs list, select one or several jobs and then select the new "Notify Users" tab, choose the users to be notified, and specify the type of information they should receive (success, error, warning).  

--
Prerequisite

In the SAP Business Technology Platform Cockpit, enable the automatic update the import status from your SAP S/4HANA system.

If the automatic update of the import status isn’t enabled, the display for jobs with ACDOCU as a target is the following:

  • The run status displays "Request Sent” until a user opens the job run of the last run. At that moment, the status is updated (same behavior as in the job editor).
  • The "imported data" micro-chart is displayed in grey. The number of rows sent is indicated as information in the tooltip.
  • The "Last Successful Run" column is only updated when a user opens the job run of the last run.

--
In brief

The new advanced monitoring and alerting features in SAP Group Reporting Data Collection’s data mapping app streamline your management of data mapping jobs, improve your ability to detect and resolve errors, and increase your overall efficiency in loading data into group reporting. By providing you with comprehensive and accessible information, you benefit from a more robust solution for maintaining data quality and improving your financial close.  

--
More information

  • SAP Group Reporting Data Collection help portal
  • Recap of group reporting new features
  • Group reporting community
  • Group reporting customer influence platform

entry.title: Re: Lock Some fields of PR through ABAP Code

entry.title: Re: Lock Some fields of PR through ABAP Code
entry.content:
Did you already look at BAdI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST, field selection methods such as FIELDSELECTION_HEADER allow to change some display field attributes. You may not be allowed to handle standard fields, but you could rise an error and restore previous value in PROCESS_HEADER.

  • More options/fields are handled in ME_PROCESS_REQ, but only SAP can implement it, so you would be required to use (divert) some standard SAP implementing class 🙃 (enhancement in an implementing class)
  • There were also some customer exits available (look for EXIT_SAPLMEREQ_*  FM in Enhancement MEREQ001)

entry.title: Re: Background RFC
entry.content:
@AbhishekSharma wrote:

once you call "START_OF_BACKGROUNDTASK" it will crate another WP which will run in Background mode.

That's wrong, it still runs in a dialog work process (START_OF_BACKGROUNDTASK is just to schedule the start of an RFC unit at a given date and time). Any ABAP code running in RFC runs in a dialog work process.

entry.title: Header table of PR_OBJ object in GTS system

entry.title: B2B Integration Factory: What is Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content, and what is this used for?
entry.content:
The article B2B Integration Factory services to unlock the power of SAP Integration Suite's B2B capabilities mentions the regular creation, management, and delivery of pre-packaged B2B integration content. This blog is about the content of the Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content, why we now regularly deliver packages, and what the benefits are for users.

 

Introduction

We, the B2B Integration Factory, now regularly offer new and enhanced Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub à Community Section for two following reasons:

  1. Compared to SAP Cloud Integration or SAP PI/PO, the new B2B capabilities of SAP Integration Suite follow a new paradigm. This new paradigm focuses on the business-orientated, semantic view while creating B2B-related integration content. The technically oriented runtime artifacts will be automatically generated from it. The following chapter Business Oriented View for B2B Integration Content Building Describes this paradigm in more detail. This pre-packaged B2B integration content can be used as templates and examples to understand and internalize this new paradigm better.
  2. This pre-packaged B2B Integration Content also represents common B2B scenarios for electronic business transactions widely used in specific industries. We identified that the degree of reuse is very high when a similar B2B scenario is prevalent in a comparable or related industry. This makes it much more efficient for you to copy and adapt it accordingly for your local tenant.

Business Oriented View for B2B Integration Content Building

The Trading Partner Agreements (TPAs) of SAP's Trading Partner Management consider B2B scenarios in which the choreography of business documents is exchanged by the agreements with the respective business partners so that the agreed collaborative business process between the involved trading partners will be successfully fulfilled. The Integration Advisor then describes the APIs of these business documents, their behavior, usage, and especially implementation rules via functional specifications, the so-called Message Implementation Guidelines (MIGs). How a source MIG and corresponding target MIG are mapped on business meaning and syntactic levels are then described in assigned Mapping Guidelines. Here, the Trading Partner Management and the Integration Advisor align to the conventions of the ISO/IEC 14662 standard "Information Technologies - Open-EDI reference model." the figure shown divides the electronic exchange of Business Data into a Business Operational View (BOV) and a Functional Service View (FSV). 7

The BOV considers the semantics of business data in business transactions and the associated data exchange that applies to business requirements between business partners. The BOV-related specifications and rules shape B2B Scenarios in the respective domains. The intention is to shape TPAs, especially the choreography of the Business Transactions in the B2B Scenario, which should align with the concepts of UMM (UN/CEFACT Modeling Methodology).

The FSV deals with the supporting services that meet the technical requirements for the runtime execution of electronic business data exchange and focuses on information technology aspects such as functional capabilities, service interfaces, technical mapping artifacts, and exchange protocols. These technical runtime artifacts will be automatically generated from the definitions and configurations in the Trading Partner Agreements and stored in the central Partner Directory, which will be dynamically obtained according to the received business data on the sender side.

In other words, the supported approach by Trading Partner Management and Integration Advisor gives you significant advantages. As a business-oriented B2B expert, you must focus on the requirements on a business-oriented level while designing trading partner agreements. Trading Partner Management and Integration Advisor then ensure the technical artifacts are automatically created from these business-oriented definitions and configurations.

What do you find in a Pre-Packaged B2B Integration Content?

The pre-packaged B2B integration content follows the paradigm of the business-oriented view. This includes:

Trading Partner Profile (TPP)

A sample Trading Partner Profile (TPP) and the corresponding sample Company Profile (CPP) with the essential information required for the trading partner agreement, such as

  • Primary overview data
  • Example identifiers
  • Example business context
  • B2B System with a required type system, such as UN/EDIFACT, and example communication channels for end-to-end simulation via an API testing tool.
  • Custom parameters, if necessary
  • Predefined number ranges

 

Trading Partner Agreement + Agreement Template

A Trading Partner Agreement (TPA) and the corresponding Agreement Template with a B2B scenario to fulfill a collaborative business process in a specific industry using electronic data interchange by the B2B Capabilities at Integration Suite.

This Trading Partner Agreement also considers a specific type of system for the company (e.g., SAP IDoc) and another particular Type System and its version for the trading partner (e.g., UN/EDIFACT D.96A).

In addition, this B2B scenario involves a choreography of one-way and two-way business transactions required to fulfill the desired collaborative business process.

Each one-way and two-way business transaction consists of Business Transaction Activities that refer to the required source and target MIGs according to their direction.

Furthermore, each Business Transaction Activity contains a predefined Mapping Guideline (MAG) that describes the mapping between the corresponding source and target MIGs.

Message Implementation Guidelines (MIG)

All the assigned MIGs cover the required semantics and structure for the given business context in which the MIG is used. The business context is a combination of the set of classifications in which the MIG is considered, such as industry, country, or trading partner role. It also covers the context of the Business Transaction Activity in which the MIG is used. These MIGs also have a meaningful description of the scope, use, and conditions.

All the MIGs used in the TPAs have a precise and consistent qualification based on the IA-supported qualification approach. This powerful qualification approach not only helps to declare the business meaning of like in the following figure shown semantically individual groups such as “Buyer” or “Supplier” that are derived from a generic group called “SG2 - Name and Address.” It also helps to make the mapping elements in MAGs much more straightforward and will be automatically considered in the validation rules.

Furthermore, all defined nodes in the MIGs have meaningful names, descriptions, and correct properties such as cardinality, primitive type, length, format, etc. Coded leaf nodes refer to the required code lists, which might be restricted if only a subset of the code values is needed.

If necessary, integrity conditions and business rules across nodes are expressed via assertions for further validation.

Mapping Guidelines (MAG)

Each Mapping Guideline is based on the associated source and target MIGs built as described in the previous chapter. Each MAG also meaningfully describes the scope, use, and conditions and has all the relevant mapping elements between the semantically corresponding source and target nodes.

Each mapping element exists of one or more of the following characteristics:

  • The primary use of the embedded no-coding features, if possible. For example, the mapping elements between date/time leaf nodes are based on the embedded “Date Time Conversion,” in which the leaf nodes' given date/time format must be set.
  • With XPath V2.0-based mapping element functions when the embedded no-coding features do not support the criteria. XPath 2.0 is a logically constructed, compelling, easy-to-learn language and a powerful function library that can be used for nearly all transformation requirements.
  • Code value mappings based on the embedded code value mapping logic.
  • Conditions that are based on the built-in condition logic.
  • Meaningful descriptions and notes about this mapping element's intention, behavior, and conditions.

These provided MAGs don’t have many if-then-else statements because the consequent use of the mentioned qualification approach has already resolved most of them. Some MAGs might have pre-transformation rules based on the embedded pre-transformation logic. This is especially required if the order of the nodes in the source payload must be sorted into the expected structure so this can be mapped to the target structure.

The behavior of these Mapping Guidelines can be directly simulated with the example values in the source MIGs.

Pre- Post-Processing Integration Flows

Some of the requirements the MAG can directly express some of the requirements. ,.Target sorting may be necessary, some tag names must be substituted, or an additional call to an external source must be made. For this purpose, you will find in the pre-packaged B2B integration content the necessary pre- or post-post-processing integration flows in a separate integration package. These pre- or post-processing integration flows are directly assigned to the appropriate Business Transaction Activity of the given Trading Partner Agreement.

How do you get and activate the Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content?

You’ll find it in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. You can discover this by searching for “B2B Integration Factory”. Apart from the listed B2B-related Integration Packages with your custom extensions, you’ll also find a list of available Pre-packaged B2B Integration Content for diverse B2B scenarios in which an electronic business data exchange between different type systems is required. You can then download the appropriate archive (zip) files in GitHub and import, activate, and test it. , as well as how to use the guide. The attached configuration and how to use the guide describe all the required steps.

All the pre-packaged B2B integration content is community content provided as a standalone component under Apache License, v.2.0. It is not part of any SAP product. Therefore, it can be locally adjusted according to the final requirements. SAP does not provide official support for all the B2B integration content offered. If you find an issue in the provided content, please raise any issue under the associated GitHub project by clicking the “Issues” button.

If you miss anything in the provided pre-packaged B2B integration content for a specific B2B scenario, you can also pull a request from GitHub via the button “Pull Request.” As the B2B Integration Factory, we regularly check all the requests and shape our future backlog according to the demand we also find here.

entry.title: Seeking Support to Achieve My Dream: SAP HCM Associate Certificate
entry.content:
As an aspiring SAP HCM professional, I am on a journey to deepen my skills and knowledge in the world of SAP. Over the past year, I have transitioned from an SAP ABAP developer role into an SAP HCM consultant position. This shift has allowed me to explore the intricate workings of Human Capital Management and develop hands-on experience with SAP's comprehensive solutions.

In my current role, I have gained valuable experience in understanding the complexities of SAP HCM, such as personnel administration, organizational management, and time management. However, to further my professional growth and truly solidify my foundation, I am keen on earning the SAP HCM Associate Certification. Achieving this certification would not only validate my skills but also open new opportunities for career advancement and allow me to contribute more effectively to my organization.

Unfortunately, due to financial constraints, I am unable to afford the cost of the certification. I am reaching out to the SAP community and certification bodies to seek support for obtaining this valuable credential. I believe that with a certification, I can maximize my potential and continue contributing to SAP HCM projects with confidence and expertise.

Why Am I a Worthy Candidate?

  • Passion for SAP HCM: I am passionate about leveraging SAP technology to optimize HR processes and improve organizational efficiency. I consistently invest time in self-learning through available online resources, webinars, and hands-on practice.

  • Practical Experience: With my background as an SAP ABAP developer and my current role as an SAP HCM consultant, I have acquired a blend of technical and functional skills. This combination enables me to approach SAP HCM problems with a holistic perspective, bridging the gap between technical development and business requirements.

  • Commitment to Professional Growth: Despite financial challenges, I remain dedicated to advancing my career and skill set. The SAP HCM certification would serve as a catalyst for my professional development and would enable me to deliver better outcomes for clients and projects.

How Can SAP and the Community Support Me?

I am requesting assistance in the form of a scholarship or sponsorship to cover the certification cost. By investing in me, SAP or any supporting organization would not only be helping an individual achieve their goals but also contributing to the growth of the SAP community by enabling a committed professional to flourish.

Thank you for taking the time to read my story. I remain hopeful that I will find a way to achieve this certification and continue my journey as an SAP HCM consultant. Your support would be greatly appreciated, and I promise to pay it forward by helping others in similar situations once I am in a position to do so.

Sincerely,
Yeswanthraj Krishnan.

entry.title: Apply Now for SAP training program at ASUE
entry.content:
The Armenian State University of Economics is launching a special SAP training program for anyone interested in becoming an SAP professional. This program will be guided by experts from local SAP partners and customers.

🎓Why join?

  • Those who successfully complete the course will have the opportunity to receive free SAP certification.
  • There may also be opportunities for internships at organizations within the local SAP ecosystem.
  • It requires no prior SAP experience—perfect for anyone looking to start their journey in the SAP world!

🗓Application deadline: October 27, 2024
📧Apply by sending your CV and LinkedIn profile to the following address: sityerevan@sap.com

entry.title: Unable to see candidate communication from another recruiting team member
entry.content:
In Recruiting Management we are adding people to the Recruiter Team once creating a Job. The recruiter and the recruiting team members have therefore access to candidates who apply to a job. The recruiter and the recruiting team members are having conversations with the candidate BUT the recruiters (whether its the recruiter or a recruiter team member) cannot see each others text messages. It can happen that the candidate is being asked the same question from different recruiter and it also leads to inefficiency among the recruiters.

How do you handle such cases?

entry.title: Re: Converting DOCX file to PDF?
entry.content:
I think that only way would be using some third party converter application.

For example you can think about installing some "converter library" on application server and calling it via the SM69 external command in ABAP. Plus of course saving DOCX & reading converted PDF files through OPEN DATASET commands on application server.

entry.title: Extract KNKK Customer Master Credit Management (Control Area) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER           

  •  Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER    
  • Tcode - Z_KNKK_CUST    
  • Entity Description - Customer Master Credit Management (Control Area) 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- RKDKLI_KNKK.CSV  
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – KNKK, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • KLIMK  

---------------ABAP Program start from here-------------------

REPORT Z_RKDKLI_KNKK_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.

PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\KNKK.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

*&------WRITE: / 'LEGAL ENTITY NAME:', ENTITY.

*&------WRITE: / 'OUTPUT FILE PATH:', OPATH.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNKK-KUNNR,
KLIMK like KNKK-KLIMK,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT DISTINCT KNKK~KUNNR
KNKK~KLIMK
KNB1~BUKRS
from KNKK INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNKK~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Re: Avoidance of goods movements for production yield and...
entry.content:
Hi,

You should be able to use a Custom XSLT in Manage Integration Workflows app for the required integration workflows, where you remove/omit goods movement segments, so they are not posted as part of the payload.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: SCIM 2.0 API - User Provisioning - Bulk Operations (BulkRequest)
entry.content:
Hi All,

Recently started investigating SCIM 2.0 API functionality for Datasphere. I managed to create a single user, as well as delete a user. So authorization wise I should not encounter any issues.

Now for bulk operations following scheme needs to be used: 

"schemas": ["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:BulkRequest"]

Running this for both the creation of users as well as for the deletion of users, it doens't seem to work. I received a 400 error ("Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema").

 
Looking a bit deeper into the list of all avaible schemas under ../scim2/schemas, I don't see the BulkRequest schema listed.
 
Anyone managed to use this BulkRequest schema and if so how?
Thanks in advance!
 
Kind Regards,
Thibault

entry.title: Re: Why was "external code" and "enabled for ESS" removed...
entry.content:
Good point, 

a new attribute was added to this object and than all the fields have been moved automatically into the details. This is unfortunately standard MDF behaviour. But agreed, this is not good. The overview should present external code and ess enabled as fields in the list.

Let us see if we can bring those back in the next release.

Best regards

Volker
